era: Hip-Hop continues post-era. E=6 -- dark cinematic force, Capitol Hill underground, self-released. Ninth consecutive E=6 (161-169). v1.49.309.

location.formative: Capitol Hill underground -- DIY venues, Bandcamp/DatPiff distribution, self-released economy. Dark cinematic aesthetic.
location.career: Nacho Picasso. Dark cinematic hip-hop. Prolific output. Self-released. 2010-present.

members.name: Nacho Picasso
members.note: Capitol Hill, Seattle. Dark cinematic hip-hop. Prolific underground output. BSBD collaborations.

associated_musicians.name: Blue Sky Black Death (BSBD)
associated_musicians.note: Young God and Kingston. Atmospheric, sample-heavy production. Key collaborative partner.

theory_nodes.concept: Dark cinematic E=6 -- hip-hop as immersive cinematic environment. Horror samples, noir aesthetics, atmospheric production creating a world the listener enters rather than just hears.
examples: Laconic delivery against dark production -- contrast as effect.
Prolific output as sustained maximum energy.
theory_nodes.description: Dark cinematic force. E=6 through atmosphere and volume of output.

genealogy.relationship: Nacho Picasso at 169. Dark cinema as force.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-released economy -- prolific output as survival strategy in the absence of label infrastructure. Volume as brand. Underground audience built through consistency.
examples: No marketing budget. Music as its own marketing.
Capitol Hill infrastructure as label substitute.
theory_nodes.description: Self-released economy. Volume as strategy. The artist is the label.

theory_nodes.concept: Post-era continuation -- genre eras as gradients, not walls. Hip-hop continues after the formal era closes at 168. The engine's genre boundaries are permeable.
examples: Era boundary as gradient, not cliff.
theory_nodes.description: Post-era continuation. Genre eras are gradients. Hip-hop persists.

cross_references.relationship: ERA TRANSITION! Gifted Gab closed the formal hip-hop era (168). Nacho Picasso continues hip-hop post-era (169). Genre gradient in action.
DARK HIP-HOP! Porter Ray: street narrative darkness. Nacho Picasso: cinematic horror darkness. Two modes of dark E=6.
UNDERGROUND FORCE! Both underground, self-released, aggressive. Kung Foo Grip: aggressive underground. Nacho Picasso: dark cinematic underground.
CAPITOL HILL HIP-HOP! Both Capitol Hill-connected hip-hop artists. Different decades, same neighborhood infrastructure.
